title: GitHub Repo Security Scanner — Investigation Form
description: Validates a completed investigation form (V1.2). Harness fills phase_1; Python computes phase_3 (7 deterministic ops) + phase_3_advisory (scorecard hints, advisory only); LLM fills phases 4-5; renderer consumes the complete form.
$comment: V1.2 — D-7 + D-8 co-scheduled schema bump per 2026-04-20 board review (docs/External-Board-Reviews/042026-schema-v12/CONSOLIDATION.md). D1: harness-canonical shapes/names (archived/fork/default_branch; nested per-family dangerous_primitives; {verified,total} for distribution_channels_verified; bare lists for install_hooks/executable_files/agent_rule_files; dependabot_alerts/osv_lookups; transformer deleted). D2: new top-level phase_3_advisory.scorecard_hints (keyed by question ID); phase_4_structured_llm.scorecard_cells authoritative with {rationale, edge_case, suggested_threshold_adjustment, computed_signal_refs, override_reason}; phase_3_computed.scorecard_cells deleted. Items A/C/E/F: override_reason 5-value enum frozen; agent_rule_files.total_bytes dropped; 23-row signal ID vocabulary.

schema_version.description: Semantic version of the form schema

action_step_type.description: Category of action step. Short forms (install, mitigate, nudge) are renderer-ready; long forms (install_normally, pin_version, ...) are the V1.0 prescriptive vocabulary kept for backwards compatibility.

command_lang.description: Code fence language for the command block (e.g. bash, yaml)

result_lang.description: Code fence language for the result block (e.g. json, yaml)

reason.description: Why this evidence is priority — per S8-7, should articulate what falsification of this claim would mean (would flip the verdict). If that can't be articulated, it isn't priority.

label.description: Beat name — max ~3 words (forbidden: SCAN, START, TODAY restatements per S8-9)

executable_file_inventory_entry.$comment: C2 FIX NOW (V2.3 R3 C3, 2026-04-18 board impl-verify R1): file_sha + line_ranges + diff_anchor are STRUCTURALLY required on every entry, with null allowed when a scan genuinely cannot resolve them. This supersedes the earlier conditional trigger (`dangerous_calls != None OR kind in sensitive set`) which left the `kind` half underspecified; Codex BLOCK at V2 + R2 dissent both preferred always-required-with-null-fallback, and owner directive on 2026-04-18 adopted that resolution. Rationale: reproducibility is uniform — every entry must declare the fields; when a value is genuinely unresolvable, the scanner emits null (not omits the key). This matches the documentary-enforcement pattern used elsewhere in the schema.

file_sha.description: C2: File SHA at scanned revision. Required key; null only when the scan cannot resolve (document the reason in notes).

line_ranges.description: C2: Relevant lines (e.g. '45-78, 120-135'). Required key; null only when not applicable (e.g. whole-file relevance, binary).

diff_anchor.description: C2: Diff anchor for reproducibility. Required key; null only when a stable anchor cannot be formed.

what.description: Short prose — what this PR did

reviewed.description: Formal review decision or 'No formal decision'

concern.description: Short concern/note or empty if routine

flagged.description: True if this row is a security-concerning PR

coverage_detail_monorepo.$comment: C4 FIX NOW (V2.3 R3 C11): Explicit monorepo coverage fields as typed sibling object — DeepSeek R2 dissent preferred extending generic rows[].

coverage_detail_pr_target_usage.$comment: C4 FIX NOW (V2.3 R3 C11): Explicit pull_request_target usage reporting — must be stated even when 0.

body_paragraphs.description: Multi-paragraph prose body. Prose-LLM output; structural constraint only.

kind.description: Optional finding kind label (e.g. 'Structural', 'Hygiene', 'Supply chain'). Rendered between severity and title.

rule_id.description: Reference to the scanner rule that fired (e.g. 'C20', 'F11').

date_label.description: Date context for the finding (e.g. 'Since repo creation', '2024-01 to present').

how_to_fix.description: Concrete maintainer-side or consumer-side fix instructions. Appears below meta_table in §02.

auto_load_tier.description: C3 FIX NOW: required on findings whose category matches agent-rule-file patterns. See allOf.

boundary_case.description: Flag indicating this finding sits near a severity threshold (see phase_3_computed.boundary_cases for the detailed case).

boundary_note.description: One-line human-readable note about the boundary (e.g. '31 days since latest release — 1 day outside the 30-day Critical window').

allOf.$comment: Evidence_refs required for any non-OK finding.

C3 FIX NOW (V2.3 R3 C9, 2026-04-18 board R3 Option X): auto_load_tier required on findings whose category matches agent-rule-file patterns. V1.2 will re-bind this to domain=='agent' per SD2 deferred roadmap (project_pipeline_architecture.md).

override_reason_enum.description: Frozen 5-value enum per V1.2 board review Item C. Categorizes why Phase 4 color diverges from Phase 3 advisory color.

advisory_signal_ref.description: A single compute.py advisory signal with stable ID + value. IDs must resolve against compute.SIGNAL_IDS (23 frozen IDs per V1.2 board review Item F).

id.description: snake_case stable ID, question-scoped prefix (q1_ / q2_ / q3_ / q4_ / c20_)

value.description: Raw signal value (bool / number / enum / object depending on signal)

scorecard_hint.description: Phase 3 advisory scorecard hint — non-authoritative. compute.py populates this; Phase 4 may override with rationale + computed_signal_refs citing these signals by ID.

scorecard_cell.description: Phase 4 authoritative scorecard cell. LLM-authored. If color differs from the paired phase_3_advisory.scorecard_hints[key].color, validator requires override_reason + computed_signal_refs non-empty + rationale ≥ 50 chars (gate 6.3 override-explained per V1.2 Item A).

rationale.description: Prose explanation of the Phase 4 color. Required non-null when overriding advisory; validator enforces ≥50 chars.

edge_case.description: Optional note flagging boundary/edge-case reasoning that informed the color.

suggested_threshold_adjustment.description: Optional note back-channel to compute.py calibration: what rubric threshold change would align Phase 3 with this Phase 4 color.

computed_signal_refs.description: List of compute.SIGNAL_IDS entries (23 frozen IDs per V1.2 Item F) the Phase 4 judgment considered. Required non-empty when overriding advisory color.

override_reason.description: Categorical override reason. Required non-null when Phase 4 color differs from Phase 3 advisory color.

symlinks_stripped.description: V1.2 D1: integer count of symlinks stripped during extraction (not boolean). 0 = none stripped.

reset.description: V1.2 D1: epoch integer (harness-native). V1.1 used reset_at ISO string.

default_branch.description: V1.2 D1: harness-canonical name (was default_branch_ref in V1.1).

archived.description: V1.2 D1: harness-canonical name (was is_archived in V1.1).

fork.description: V1.2 D1: harness-canonical name (was is_fork in V1.1).

open_issues_count.description: V1.2 D1: harness already emits this; schema now accepts.

size_kb.description: V1.2 D1: harness already emits this; schema now accepts.

branch_protection.description: V1.2 D1: inner sub-objects (classic/rulesets/rules_on_default/org_rulesets) accept harness-native fields (body, count, etc.) in addition to the named properties. Transformer's strip-to-{entries, raw_response} shape deleted.

status.description: HTTP status code from branch protection API. 404 = no protection; 200 = configured; 403 = token lacks scope.

locations_checked.description: All paths checked for CODEOWNERS file (standard four: CODEOWNERS, .github/CODEOWNERS, docs/CODEOWNERS, .gitlab/CODEOWNERS).

manifest_files.description: V1.2 D1: harness-native shape — array of {path, format} objects (was array of strings in V1.1).

dependabot_alerts.description: V1.2 D1: renamed from dependabot in V1.1; harness-canonical.

osv_lookups.description: V1.2 D1: renamed from osv_dev in V1.1; harness emits per-package array directly.

dependabot_config.description: V1.2 D1: parsed .github/dependabot.yml contents (schedule, open-pr-limit, etc.). New in V1.2 — harness already gathers.

dangerous_primitives.description: V1.2 D1: harness-native nested per-family shape. 15 pattern families (exec/deserialization/network/secrets/vendor_keys/tls_cors/sql_injection/cmd_injection/auth_bypass/path_traversal/archive_unsafe/ssrf/xss/weak_crypto/debug_flags). Each family has {scanned: bool, files: [{file, line_count, first_match: {line, snippet}}]}. $comment retained for noting unscanned reasons. NOTE: no renderer consumer as of V1.2; renderer V1.3 should consume nested shape directly, not re-flatten (Item D).

executable_files.description: V1.2 D1: bare list (was {entries: []} wrapper in V1.1). Harness-native.

install_hooks.description: V1.2 D1: bare list (was {found, hooks} wrapper in V1.1). Empty list = no hooks detected.

readme_paste_blocks.description: V1.2 D1: harness-native shape with entries_count + entries (was {found, blocks} wrapper in V1.1).

agent_rule_files.description: V1.2 D1 + Item E: bare list (was wrapper in V1.1). total_bytes DROPPED. Each entry has {path, kind, line_count, ci_amplified, ...} per-entry shape.

total_merged_lifetime.description: V1.2 D1: harness-emitted; total merged PRs across repo lifetime (denominator for rate-over-time).

self_merge_count.description: V1.2 D1: harness-emitted absolute count of self-merged PRs.

security_flagged_count.description: V1.2 D1: harness-emitted count of security-flagged PRs in the sample.

security_flagged_numbers.description: V1.2 D1: harness-emitted list of PR numbers flagged as security-relevant.

http_status.description: V1.2 D1: transport-level HTTP status from api.securityscorecards.dev. Useful for diagnosing API failures (404 = not indexed, 503 = transient). Preserved per DeepSeek R1 I-2.

windows_surface_coverage.description: V1.2 D1: harness emits status string ("scanned" / "skipped" / etc.). V1.1 accepted only bool; V1.2 accepts both during transition.

distribution_channels_verified.description: V1.2 D1 + P2-LOSSY-CAST fix: richer than V1.1's bool-only cast. Harness can emit {verified, total} object or fraction string; bool retained for backwards-compat.

phase_3_advisory.$comment: V1.2 D2: new top-level phase for advisory-only Python output. compute.compute_scorecard_advisory() populates scorecard_hints; these are NON-authoritative. Phase 4 is the authoritative scorecard source. When Phase 4 color differs from the paired Phase 3 advisory color, validator (gate 6.3) enforces override-explained: rationale + computed_signal_refs + override_reason enum.

scorecard_hints.description: Keyed object by canonical question ID (OQ-4 unanimous: keyed not array).

scorecard_cells.$comment: V1.2 D2: authoritative scorecard cells — LLM-authored. Keyed object by canonical question ID (OQ-4 unanimous). Moved from phase_3_computed per D-7 board decision. When Phase 4 color diverges from phase_3_advisory.scorecard_hints[key].color, gate 6.3 override-explained requires: rationale ≥50 chars, computed_signal_refs non-empty with all IDs resolving against compute.SIGNAL_IDS, override_reason in 5-value enum.

section_leads.$comment: Per-section pill_row + summary blockquotes rendered at top of §01/§02/etc.

verdict_exhibits.$comment: Detailed exhibit groups for the verdict section (§Verdict). See §02 and S8-3 (7+ similar-severity items roll up into exhibits).

evidence.$comment: All evidence entries for §07 Evidence Appendix. Each entry is command-backed; priority selections reference these by id.

entries.description: Short prose bullets listing gaps noted in coverage (e.g. 'Artifact reproducibility not verified').

prior_scan_note.description: Optional note rendered in the Catalog row 'Prior scan' — e.g. 'None — first scan; rename this file on re-run.'
